extra.message: User-controlled input from the `username` query parameter is concatenated directly into a Jinja2 template string via `Environment().from_string(...)` and then rendered. This allows Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI): an attacker can inject Jinja2 expressions (e.g. `{{7*7}}` or payloads accessing `__class__`/`__mro__`/`__subclasses__`) to read sensitive data or achieve remote code execution. The safe pattern is to pass the value as a template variable instead of concatenating it into the template source.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message: The `username` query parameter is rendered into an HTML response without any escaping and returned via `HTMLResponse`. Because Jinja2's `Environment()` is created without autoescape and the value is injected into the raw template source, any HTML/JavaScript payload (e.g. `<script>alert(1)</script>`) supplied by the user will be reflected back to the browser, enabling reflected Cross-Site Scripting.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
